comment.body:
Always be patient, and avoid trading for only the sake of it.
Without conception, without plan, without thinking, there is no longer-term growth. 
Be patient with the growth.
Market won't let you to be millionaire overnight.

Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.
This is a mental trap. I tell you what I mean by that.
You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...

at the very beginning this is true

comment.body:
so for example, you short a shorter-dated contract, and long a longer-dated one... you have net positive vega
bcs the shorted dated one's vega accelerates faster that the longer dated one, your PnL can slow down significantly, stagnate or even start to decrease at some point

comment.body: Much traditional understanding is that dollar weakness causes stocks to go lower (input prices go up). Why has this huge run been driven by it?

comment.body: A weaker U.S. dollar leads to higher nominal dollar-denominated sales, production, and services—or increased volume (or both)—resulting in higher corporate revenue and an anticipated improvement in the next quarter's earnings report.

comment.body:
Think im seeing whats happening after reviewing some stuff (also thanks to everyone for answering my questions yesterday). 
So in this regime, higher vol pushes the market higher, lower vol would actually drop it. But due to the high vol of vol supply (overwriting of put options by funds) it actually compresses the move. MM's to downside are long vega and with a high supply you get a larger increase in vega. So that means they will compress volatility to the downside aggressivly. 

So wtf is happening? Looking at ll the gaps, its likely CTA's and vol regimes. One naive way of looking at how these trend following algos do it (and this is what im doing until i get a better grasp on it is). One way to think about it is  If above 20,50,100SMA then buy 10shares X (3/2/1). For vol funds if HV10 less than 15 then do the same, again really naive way of looking at it. The gaps are the telling part. So as alma was saying was vanna is flattening 
This is what i have. So i think thats what she means by regime is changing and we won't actually go down until then.  I think the second image is what you'd want to see for any bearish bias
